findings.design_reference: FR-009; workshop integration/API and observability/resilience lenses; .review/process/process-context.md
findings.comment: Write-ContinuousCoReviewProcessContext derives the active feature only from stripped governance state and silently emits an empty `Active feature:` when that lookup is unavailable. The reviewed packet demonstrates the failure (`.review/process/process-context.md` is blank for Active feature) even though specs/198-beta2-hardening/iterations/003/state.md and plan.md clearly identify the active increment. This prevents a fresh reviewer from reliably selecting the applicable plan/tasks/drift records and undermines the mandated process/progress review. Derive the feature/iteration from a worktree-visible authoritative input or fail loudly when the curated context cannot identify it, and add a packet-level regression asserting a non-empty, correct active feature and iteration.

findings.design_reference: FR-015 evidence integrity; NFR honesty-first; process progress honesty rule
findings.comment: The state claims the digest-linked bounded-verification suite passed `12/12`, but the digest-matched `.review/implementer-evidence.json` records 11 passed/0 failed and coverage-evidence.md:31 also says 11. This is a direct mismatch between the progress narrative and runner-observed evidence; the adjacent aggregate `Suites 24/24` claim is likewise not derivable from the visible 11+7 results. Correct the state/count aggregates to the runner-observed values (or supply coherent runner-observed evidence for any additional tests) before treating T015 evidence as honest.

The reviewer integrity hash excludes the entire .antigravitycli/.codex/.claude/.cursor/.gemini/.copilot directory trees as volatile. Those trees can contain tracked project source/configuration (this reviewed tree itself contains .codex/agents/*.toml, .claude/settings.local.json, and .copilot/mcp-config.json), so a reviewer can modify or delete them and the before/after maps will remain equal. This contradicts the binding strict-read-only contract and the orchestrator's claim that only .review/findings.jsonl may change. Exclude only specifically identified runtime artifacts (or distinguish tracked snapshot files from newly created host runtime state) and add a paired test that mutation of a pre-existing tracked host-directory file fails the run.

findings.design_reference: NFR-007 / requirements-nfr lens / tasks.md T013,T014,T015,T020 / process progress honesty rule
findings.comment: The iteration marks T013, T014, T015, and T020 done and this hand-authored file claims the orchestrator-integrity suite and the 16-suite registry were run for real and green, but the digest-matched implementer evidence supplied for this exact reviewed tree records only bounded-verification.Tests.ps1 (11/0). The prompt explicitly gives hand-authored quality notes zero evidence standing, so there is no traceable runtime basis here for the completed T013/T014/T020 claims or for the production-path integrity behavior central to T015; merely listing commands and counts in prose does not cure that gap. Record runner-produced, digest-matched evidence for the targeted containment, origin-hygiene, reviewer-integrity, and spend-allowance suites (or revert the completion/status claims until such evidence exists).
